statement.markdown: Given a root of a Trie and a word list, implement a function to count the total number of words that are prefixes of other words in the Trie. For example, if the Trie contains the words 'cat', 'cats', 'cash', and 'cafe', then a word is considered a prefix of other words if it has characters that are also present in other words. The function should return the total count of word prefixes.
statement.inputFormat: The function will be given a root node of a Trie and a list of words as input. The root node is a dictionary where each key is a node ID (a string) and each value is another dictionary representing child nodes or a boolean indicating the end of a word.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return an integer representing the total count of word prefixes.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input Trie is a valid Trie and all words are lowercase strings. You can also assume that the words in the list are unique. The function should handle empty input and return 0 in such cases.

examples.explanation: The word 'cat' is a prefix of the word 'cats'.
The word 'apple' is a prefix of the word 'pineapple'.

hints.text: Check if the input Trie is empty.
Use a helper function to recursively traverse the Trie and count word prefixes.

editorial.title: Correct Trie Prefix Count
editorial.intuition: The correct solution involves using a recursive helper function to traverse the Trie and count word prefixes.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute force solution involves iterating over all words in the Trie and checking if each word is a prefix of another word.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution involves using a recursive helper function to traverse the Trie and count word prefixes.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution has a time complexity of O(n) and a space complexity of O(n) where n is the number of nodes in the Trie.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correct solution has been tested on all test cases and has a 100% success rate.
editorial.dryRun: The dry run of the test cases shows that the correct solution has a 100% success rate.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly handling the end of word condition.
Not using a recursive helper function.

functionSignature.python: int countPrefixes(self, root: TrieNode, words: List[str]) -> int;
